title: Unable to Print
shortDescription: One or more ink absorber pads are nearly full.
fullDescription: Error 46 on Brother printers indicates the internal waste ink absorber is nearly full. The printer will eventually stop functioning to prevent ink overflow.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug it for 60 seconds, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for Jams
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and remove any jammed paper or debris.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Consumables
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall toner cartridges, drum units, or ink cartridges.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check Brother's support site for firmware updates for your model.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: What does Brother error 46 mean?
faq.a: One or more ink absorber pads are nearly full.

faq.q: Is this a common Brother error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error on Brother printers.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: Most Brother errors can be resolved with basic troubleshooting steps.

title: Printhead Cleaning Failed
shortDescription: The printhead cleaning cycle could not complete successfully.
fullDescription: Clean Unable indicates the automatic printhead cleaning process failed, often due to a severely clogged printhead or empty ink.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug it for 60 seconds, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for Jams
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and remove any jammed paper or debris.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Consumables
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall toner cartridges, drum units, or ink cartridges.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check Brother's support site for firmware updates for your model.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: What does Brother error Clean Unable mean?
faq.a: The printhead cleaning cycle could not complete successfully.

faq.q: Is this a common Brother error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error on Brother printers.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: Most Brother errors can be resolved with basic troubleshooting steps.

title: Incompatible Cartridge Installed
shortDescription: The installed cartridge is not compatible with this printer model.
fullDescription: Wrong Cartridge error means an ink or toner cartridge installed is not the correct type for this specific printer model.

fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug it for 60 seconds, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for Jams

fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall toner cartridges, drum units, or ink cartridges.

fixSteps.instruction: Check Brother's support site for firmware updates for your model.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

title: Manual Feed Required
shortDescription: The printer is waiting for paper to be placed in the manual feed slot.
fullDescription: Manual Feed message appears when the print job is configured to use the manual feed slot instead of the paper tray.

causes.cause: Print driver set to manual feed

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug it for 60 seconds, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check for Jams
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and remove any jammed paper or debris.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Consumables
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall toner cartridges, drum units, or ink cartridges.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check Brother's support site for firmware updates for your model.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.
